text: Under focusing B-NLS with kappa=+1 and IC N0=2.0*exp(-(x+5)^2/2.25), phi0=0.3*x, u0=0.3*N0 on Mcs, does the Gaussian density packet shed >=2 well-separated bright solitons (modulational instability emission) by T=6 with mass drift <5%, or does the Burgers/boost coupling alter the threshold? Phenomenon target: final N has >=2 peaks each with amplitude >=1.0.

ic: kappa=1, N0=2.0*exp(-(x+5)^2/2.25), phi0=0.3*x (non-periodic linear part), u0=0.3*N0 on Mcs. Domain [-15,15], Nx=256.
method: Madelung-Psi Strang split-step Fourier on Psi=sqrt(N)*exp(i*phi); phi-split phi=c*x+phi_tilde with c=0.3 (kb-nls-split-linear-phase); boost absorbed analytically into Psi(x,0)=sqrt(N0)*exp(i*0.3*x); equation integrated as standard-NLS form i*Psi_t=-(1/2)Psi_xx-kappa|Psi|^2*Psi (sign convention hypothesis per kb-nls-sign-convention); reconstruct u=Im(conj(Psi)*Psi_x) at snapshots (kb-nls-madelung-psi-structural-coupling). NO dealiasing at E1 (simplicity rule).
bank_use_rationale: Direct (N,phi) is known to fail family-level, so simplest *meaningful* baseline is Madelung-Psi Strang (default from kb-nls-recommended-default-bnls but de-stacked: Nx=256 per output spec, no dealiasing, single split). IC has phi=0.3*x non-periodic so phi-split is structurally required, not optional. Standard-NLS sign adopted as working hypothesis per kb-nls-sign-convention. Rejected: dealiasing (E1 must be simplest), MUSCL (no shock in IC; u=0.3*N is smooth), anti-periodic basis (IC is bright-soliton-like, periodic at edges), hard-floor regularization (counter-productive on spectral), Lie split (uneconomical), ETD-RK1/RK4 (mass-destroying).

interpretation: Method completed cleanly: mass to 1e-13, Strang-quality energy drift to 1e-8, ||m|| at machine precision (Madelung-Psi identity), excellent spectral tail (4.9e-13) so the un-dealiased Nx=256 grid is *spectrally* well-resolved for this IC. The dynamics show a TWO-soliton bound state (breather): N_max oscillates between ~2 (separated configuration at t=2.5-4, t=6) and ~11-13 (collapsed single-peak configuration at t=1.5, t=5). Final snapshot has 2 peaks both at N=2.16 separated by 1.52 in x — narrowly meets the phenomenon target (>=2 peaks, amplitude >=1.0). Caveat: IC A*sigma=3.0 matches S3's A=3/sigma=1 case (kb-nls-karpman-maslov-upper-bound, kb-nls-resolution-soliton-counting): at converged resolution S3 reports 3 solitons there, while under-resolved Nx=256 reports 3 (correctly) but with mis-estimated Linf. Here the breather oscillation period (~5 time units between collapse events) is much shorter than T=6, so the *final-time* peak count is not the same as the soliton number. Risk: Nx=256 may artifactually split a single high breather peak into two close peaks via dx-undersampling of FWHM~1/A_peak features, even with low spectral tail.
useful_self_assessment_reason: phenomenon target met as stated (2 peaks, both N>=1.0, separated), but at threshold and at the resolution warned against. Needs verification at higher resolution or against E2 to confirm the 2-peak structure is not a Nx=256 artifact.

rationale: F1 satisfies phenomenon target at face value but tightly bound 2-soliton breather at boundary of resolution warning (kb-nls-resolution-soliton-counting). To confirm result is physical and not Nx=256 dx-aliasing artifact, run E2 with ONE upgrade: add 2/3 dealiasing on |Psi|^2 before cubic exponential. This is the single component most directly relevant for high-amplitude breathers per kb-nls-23-dealiasing-cubic (without dealiasing, MI cascade can inflate spurious peaks). Keep same Nx=256 (output spec) and same dt, so any difference between E1 and E2 isolates the dealiasing effect.

method: E1 method (Madelung-Psi Strang split-step Fourier with phi-split) PLUS 2/3-rule dealiasing on |Psi|^2 (set upper 1/3 modes of |Psi|^2 to zero before pointwise cubic exponentiation) AND on the linear FFT step. Single upgrade over E1.
bank_use_rationale: Single upgrade per progressive-complexity discipline: add 2/3 dealiasing on |Psi|^2 before cubic exponential. kb-nls-23-dealiasing-cubic directly applies (high-amplitude breather, MI cascade risk; S7 had |Psi|^2 mass inflate 1e3 without dealiasing). kb-nls-resolution-soliton-counting flags Nx=256/A>=2 Gaussians as silent over-counters even without spectral cascade — dealiasing is the cheap mitigation. Rejected: anti-periodic (bright not dark), MUSCL (no shock), hard-floor (counter-productive), Lie (uneconomical), ETD (mass-destroying). Did NOT decrease dt because energy drift in E1 (1e-8) is already well below the resolution warning level; further dt halving wouldn't address the dx-aliasing concern.

interpretation: Dealiased Strang reproduces E1 to 0.01% on peak amplitudes and exactly on peak positions (-3.984, -2.461). N_max trajectory matches E1 within 0.4% throughout the breather oscillation. Mass drift slightly worse (-6.5e-6 vs +3.3e-13) because dealiasing of |Psi|^2 introduces a small non-conservation, but well below the <5% target. The 2-peak structure at t=6 is NOT a spectral-aliasing artifact (kb-nls-23-dealiasing-cubic mitigated and result unchanged). Spectral tail is below floating-point precision (8.9e-21), confirming the grid is spectrally well-resolved for this IC at Nx=256. Remaining concern: dx-undersampling of the breather's collapsed phase (kb-nls-resolution-soliton-counting; N_max=13 has FWHM~0.27 vs dx=0.117 i.e. only ~3 grid points across the collapsed peak). Need a resolution check.
useful_self_assessment_reason: Result is consistent under dealiasing upgrade, strengthens confidence in 2-peak target. But Nx=256 dx-resolution warning remains untested; high-N breather collapse not resolved by dealiasing alone.

rationale: F1 and F2 agree on the qualitative phenomenon (2-soliton breather with 2 peaks at t=6 meeting the >=1.0 threshold). To verify against kb-nls-resolution-soliton-counting (Nx=256 silent dx-aliasing on Gaussians with A>=2), E3 doubles spatial resolution to Nx=512 and halves dt to 5e-4 (single conceptual upgrade: resolution refinement). Output spec requires Nx=256, so downsample snapshots from Nx=512 -> Nx=256 via Fourier projection at save time. Keep dealiasing from E2 (carried forward; we are LAYERING resolution onto E2, not stripping its dealias). E3's purpose: confirm at higher resolution that the breather's final 2-peak structure persists with similar amplitudes.

ic: Same IC, re-discretized on Nx_fine=512 (interim grid) on x in [-15,15]; saved to Nx=256 via Fourier projection.
method: E2 method (Madelung-Psi Strang split-step Fourier + 2/3 dealias + phi-split absorbed into Psi) with INCREASED resolution: Nx_fine=512, dt=5e-4. Snapshots saved by truncating Psi_hat to lowest 256 modes (Fourier downsample). Single upgrade over E2 (resolution refinement). All other choices identical.
bank_use_rationale: Single-component upgrade: doubling Nx_fine and halving dt. kb-nls-resolution-soliton-counting directly motivates Nx>=512 for A>=2 Gaussians (S3 reported silent over-counting at Nx=256/dt=1e-3); kb-nls-cfl-split-step gives the matched dt at the new Nx (pi^2*512^2*5e-4/(2*900)=0.359, same budget). kb-nls-recommended-default-bnls endorses Nx=512+/dt=2.5e-4 as default for B-NLS. We use Nx_save=256 only at SNAPSHOT time (downsample via spectral truncation) to comply with the output spec.
